description: Chest pain in a 42-year-old smoker without classic ACS triad. Defensible to assign ESI 2 (cautious) or 3 (low-risk workup).

patient.name: Mike T., 42M
patient.chief_complaint: Intermittent left-sided chest discomfort for 2 hours, dull pressure, not radiating, mildly worse with deep breath. No diaphoresis. Mild anxiety. Drove self in.
patient.history: Smoker 15pk/yr, otherwise healthy, no known cardiac disease, father had MI age 60

rubric_notes: Chest pain in a smoker with family history of MI = ACS cannot be excluded. But the presentation is atypical (pleuritic, non-radiating, no diaphoresis, ambulatory). Vitals stable. Defensible to assign ESI 2 (low-threshold cardiac workup, observation, serial troponins) or ESI 3 (lower-risk pathway, ECG + single troponin then discharge if negative). Many EDs default to ESI 2 for any 'chest pain' in middle-aged smokers; some use HEART score to triage. Both defensible.
